summary: The packet's 18-file scope claim does not match the actual .opencode/skills diff/status surface.
Completion claims say all changed files validate, but sk-doc fails on three modified reference docs.
The shared contract cites several required files as bare filenames from shared/, which do not resolve as paths.
The shared contract mostly cites owning mode documents instead of restating their full bodies.
Router blocks and frontmatter are intact, but the new gates are mostly prose and not added to machine router maps.
Validation failures from iteration 2 remain the active sk-doc gate blocker.
All four misses have named mechanisms, but each is partly advisory unless router/dispatch enforcement is made executable.
Research artifacts are present, but stale template-count language and placement create drift risk.
The MiniMax manual scenario uses a dangerous permission bypass and contradicts the MiniMax profile's variant guidance.
No new P0/P1 surfaced in the stabilization pass; active P1 findings still require remediation before release.
